archive_summary.title: All Categories
archive_summary.description: Complete category definitions with full detailed descriptions.

section.title: All Categories (Full Detail)
section.content:
Categories: 7
## Audio Datasets ID: audio-datasets Version: 1
Covers tasks that source, clean, and version audio data from Rezolve production sessions. This includes mining production logs for voice clips, annotating ground-truth transcripts, tagging entity spans and utterance categories, and managing large audio files via DVC against the Azure Blob remote. The gold-92 benchmark dataset belongs to this category. Tasks here produce the data assets consumed by stt-evaluation, entity-correction, and whisper-finetuning tasks.
## Commercial APIs ID: commercial-apis Version: 1
Covers tasks that call, configure, and measure commercial speech-to-text services. The main subject is Deepgram Nova-2, which is the current Rezolve production STT endpoint. Work in this category establishes the baseline accuracy and latency numbers that all open-source and fine-tuned approaches are compared against. It may also include evaluation of other commercial options (e.g., Azure Speech, Google STT) if they become relevant to the research roadmap.
## Confidence Routing ID: confidence-routing Version: 1
Covers tasks that design and evaluate policies for acting on STT output with varying reliability. A routing policy maps confidence scores (and optionally entity-level scores) to one of four outcomes: accept the transcript, apply post-correction, ask the user for clarification, or hand off to a fallback path. The key objectives are keeping the wrong-confident-action rate below 2% and the clarification rate below 5% on the gold-92 benchmark without sacrificing throughput.
## Entity Correction ID: entity-correction Version: 1
Covers work that takes a raw ASR transcript and applies a correction pass to fix brand names, product lines, SKUs, and other Rezolve-specific entities. Techniques include LLM correction passes, custom vocabulary injection, and contextual entity boosting. Tasks here focus on accuracy gains measured on action-critical spans of the gold-92 benchmark and on the incremental latency cost each approach adds to the voice-to-action pipeline.
## Latency Profiling ID: latency-profiling Version: 1
Covers tasks that instrument and measure the full latency path: speech capture end, ASR decoding, optional post-correction, confidence routing, and downstream tool call dispatch. The success threshold is p50 < 800 ms. Tasks here profile individual stages, compare latency across candidate systems, and identify bottlenecks. Latency data is collected alongside accuracy metrics so accuracy–latency trade-offs can be reasoned about explicitly.
## STT Evaluation ID: stt-evaluation Version: 1
Covers tasks that measure how well a speech-to-text system transcribes Rezolve production audio. Primary metrics are entity accuracy on action-critical spans, word error rate, and intent preservation, evaluated against the gold-92 benchmark. This category includes initial baseline runs, cross-system comparisons, and regression checks. It does not include tasks that modify the STT pipeline itself — see entity-correction, confidence-routing, and whisper-finetuning for those.
## Whisper Fine-Tuning ID: whisper-finetuning Version: 1
Covers experiments that adapt pre-trained Whisper (or Whisper-derived) models to the Rezolve voice commerce domain. Tasks include dataset preparation from production audio, supervised fine-tuning runs, hyperparameter sweeps, and post-fine-tune evaluation on the gold-92 benchmark. A key constraint is that fine-tuned models must not regress general English WER relative to the base checkpoint. Gold-92 is never used for training — only for held-out evaluation.
